note:
无论你是在幼儿园，还是准备进入大学攻读本科课程，都有几家初创公司以负担得起的价格提供过多的课程来帮助这些学生实现目标。
如今，Byju‘s、Unacademy和Vedantu等当地初创公司每年帮助数千万名学生接触到备受瞩目的知名教师，以及许多人在线下环境下可能无法找到的学习材料存储库。
这些初创公司-以及传统的教育机构-正在帮助学生追逐一些最有抱负的工作：工程和医学职业。
然而，这些学生中的大多数要么最终得不到他们梦寐以求的工作--要么基于他们的技能和印度不断增长的失业数据，这完全是一份工作。
在印度，大约有4亿人，约占全国人口的三分之一，正面临着一个根本性的挑战：不会说英语，而且缺乏其他技能，这些技能在他们求职时可能会被证明是至关重要的。
总部位于南部城市高知的初创公司Entri正试图解决这一市场。这家成立三年的初创公司提供技能提升课程，帮助人们在考试中脱颖而出，这些考试可以让他们在州和联邦政府找到一份工作。它用他们最熟悉的语言教授这些课程。
在Entri的用户中，绝大多数是高中前辍学的学生，而那些已经获得研究生学位的学生，
Entri的联合创始人兼首席执行官穆罕默德·希萨穆丁(Mohammed Hisamuddin)解释说，这家初创公司的课程始于马来亚语，这是一种在印度约有5000万人说的语言，在印度南部特别受欢迎。自那以后，它增加了其他几种语言的课程，包括印地语、泰卢固语、卡纳达语和泰米尔语。
多年来，Entri还扩大了课程目录，以帮助人们寻求其他类型的工作，包括蓝领类别的工作，复制了与总部位于旧金山的Udemy类似的模式。
他说：“我们很快意识到，在参加这些考试的人中，只有大约1.5%到2%的人能够进入入围名单。”“这些考试竞争非常激烈，所以很多人开始探索私营部门的工作，有时甚至在他们已经有了一些低调工作的情况下也是如此。”
这家创业公司现在提供150多门课程，包括几种语言、会计，以及那些教授流行计算机应用程序的课程，如Microsoft Office。这些预先录制的视频课程和测验为期30至60天。
风险投资公司Good Capital的合伙人阿尔琼·马尔霍特拉(Arjun Malhotra)表示：“从每年申请政府职位的1亿人开始，Entri正在通过用他们自己的语言技能来扩大可聘用候选人的范围-这是理所应当的。”经济机会因为学习媒介而受到瓶颈，这是荒谬的。带来就业能力的技能不应该要求人们精通英语。
Hisamuddin表示，Entri在其平台上积累了300多万用户，高于今年早些时候的150万。这些用户中约有9万是付费订户。本周早些时候，他在接受TechCrunch采访时表示：“我们现在每个月都在增加近1万名付费用户。”
Entri免费提供某些语言的部分课程，但完全访问需要订阅。Hisamuddin说，付费订阅起价低至每年300印度卢比(4美元)，高达1万印度卢比(133美元)。最受欢迎的订阅层价格为1500印度卢比(20美元)。
这家初创公司本周表示，它已经完成了由Good Capital牵头的310万美元的首轮融资。在线杂货初创公司BigBasket的人力资源主管Hari TN和HyperTrack创始人Kashyap Deorah也参加了这轮融资。
它计划将新资本用于在其平台上推出50门额外的课程，并接触到更多的用户。Hisamuddin称，Entri过去三个月营收大增150%，年度经常性营收(ARR)达到200万美元。他的目标是到今年将Entri的ARR扩大到500万美元。
note_en:
Whether one is in kindergarten, or preparing to join a college to pursue an undergraduate course, there are several startups offering a plethora of courses at affordable price points to help these students get there.
 Byju’s, Unacademy, and Vedantu among other local startups today help tens of millions of students each year gain access to high-profile and established teachers and a repository of study material that many might not have been able to find in an offline setting.
  These startups — and legacy educational institutions — are helping students chase some of the most aspirational jobs: Career in engineering, and medicine.
 Most of these students, however, will either end up not getting their dream job — or based on their skills and India’s growing unemployment figures, a job altogether.
 There are about 400 million people in India, or roughly a third of the country’s population, who are confronting a fundamental challenge: Not able to speak English, and lack other skills that could prove crucial when they apply for a job.
 Entri, a startup based in the Southern city of Kochi, is attempting to address this market. The three-year-old startup offers upskilling courses to help people excel at exams that would land them a job with state and federal governments. And it teaches them these courses in the language they are most comfortable with.
 Students who dropped out before high-school to those who have already attained graduate-level degrees account for the vast majority of users of Entri,
 The startup began its courses in Malayalam, a language spoken by about 50 million people in India and especially popular in South India, explained Mohammed Hisamuddin, co-founder and chief executive of Entri. It has since added its courses in several other languages including Hindi, Telugu, Kannada, and Tamil.
 Over the years, Entri has also expanded its course catalog to help people pursuing other kinds of jobs including those in blue-collar category, replicating a model similar to that of San Francisco-headquartered  Udemy  .
  “We soon realized that only about 1.5 to 2% of the people who appear in these exams are able to make the shortlist,” he said. “These exams are very competitive, so many start to explore jobs in the private sector, sometimes even when they already have some low-profile job.”
 The startup now offers more than 150 courses, including several languages, accounting, and those that teach popular computer applications such as Microsoft Office. These pre-recorded video courses and quizzes run for 30 to 60 days.
 “Starting with the 100 million people who apply for government jobs each year, Entri is expanding the universe of employable candidates by skilling people in their own language — as it should be,” said Arjun Malhotra, a partner at venture firm Good Capital. It’s ridiculous that economic opportunities are bottlenecked because of the medium of learning. Skills bringing employability shouldn’t require people to be proficient in English.
 Hisamuddin said Entri has amassed over 3 million users on its platform, up from 1.5 million early this year. About 90,000 of these users are paying subscribers. “We are adding close to 10,000 paying subscribers each month now,” he said in an interview with TechCrunch early this week.
 Entri offers about a portion of its courses in certain languages at no charge, but complete access requires a subscription. Paid subscription starts at as low as 300 Indian rupees a year ($4) and goes as high as 10,000 Indian rupees ($133), said Hisamuddin. The most popular subscription tier costs 1,500 Indian rupees ($20).
 The startup said this week that it had closed a $3.1 million Pre-Series A financing round, led by Good Capital. Hari TN, head of human resources at online grocery startup BigBasket, and HyperTrack founder Kashyap Deorah also participated in the round.
 It plans to deploy the fresh capital into introducing 50 additional courses to its platform and reach more users. Hisamuddin said Entri’s revenues have surged 150% in the last three months and its annual recurring revenue (ARR) has reached $2 million. He aims to scale Entri’s ARR to $5 million by this year.
title: Entri筹集310万美元建造白话语言“印度的Udemy”
title_en: Entri raises $3.1M to build a vernacular language ‘Udemy for India’

note:
如果你多年来一直在关注英特尔从14 nm制程到10 nm制程的问题，你可能不会对该公司现在7 nm制程遇到困难感到惊讶。据Tom‘s Hardware报道，在今天的第二季度财报电话会议上，英特尔透露，它将把之前计划的7 nm推出推迟6个月-这一过程的产量现在比计划晚了一年。这意味着英特尔目前无法以经济可行的方式生产7纳米芯片。
英特尔原本预计在2021年赶上AMD的7纳米芯片，但没有透露2021年的时间。有了这些新的延迟，英特尔的7 nm芯片最早将于2022年亮相。根据AMD的路线图，到那时，AMD可能已经在其基于Zen 4架构的Ryzen 6000 5 nm芯片上了-尽管这是假设AMD本身不会遇到任何延迟。
然而，在10 nm方面有一些好消息。在英特尔2020年第二季度的新闻稿中，该公司表示，它正在“今年加快向10纳米产品的过渡”，并扩大其基于10纳米的英特尔酷睿处理器的产品组合。这包括它的Tiger Lake芯片和第一个基于10 nm的服务器CPU Ice Lake。此外，英特尔表示，“预计将交付一系列新的客户端CPU(代号为Alder Lake)，其中将包括其首款基于10 nm的台式CPU，以及一款新的基于10 nm的服务器CPU(代号为Sapphire Rapids)。”
英特尔最初在2015年宣布了其10 nm芯片，但证实当年7月存在产量问题和其他问题。从那时起，其首批10 nm的Cannon Lake芯片本应在2017年下半年首次亮相，但到2018年4月，英特尔的产量仍然很低，因此仍然没有芯片。到2019年，英特尔已经改进了10纳米的制造工艺，开始看到更好的结果，并表示将在2019年假期前“上架”。但是，除了配备Iris Pro Graphics的处理器外，我们还没有配备10纳米芯片的消费级笔记本电脑或台式机。第10代英特尔移动和台式机处理器仍然使用14纳米。我们会在2020年前看到10纳米芯片吗？根据英特尔在财报电话会议上的说法，这是有可能的。
今天，英特尔宣布了其新的第10代U系列和Y系列CPU产品阵容。等。英特尔…不是。
多读。
但更重要的是，英特尔预计其10 nm节点不会像14 nm节点那样好。英特尔首席财务官乔治·戴维斯在3月份摩根士丹利(Morgan Stanley)的一次金融分析师活动上承认了这一点：
“这不会是英特尔有史以来最好的节点。它的生产效率将低于14[纳米]，低于22[纳米]…。事实是，就像我说的，它不会像人们预期的14纳米或7纳米时那样结实。“
但是7 nm会是一个强大的节点吗？该公司遇到的许多与10 nm相同的问题似乎又出现了，如果这是历史重演的情况，那么可能是2025年，直到我们看到英特尔推出7 nm芯片。这是最坏的情况，听起来不像英特尔会支持的东西，特别是考虑到目前来自AMD的激烈竞争。该公司还失去了一些知名员工，比如最近担任英特尔硅工程集团高级副总裁的吉姆·凯勒(Jim Keller)。
但与此同时，英特尔的10纳米芯片有望略微超过其第10代处理器。不幸的是，通过目前14 nm节点上的高端内核，已经没有多少可获得的了。酷睿i9-10900K的最大升压时钟只比酷睿i9-9900K高300 MHz(5.3 GHz比5.0 GHz)，这是因为英特尔的热速度提升和涡轮增强技术-但这些提升只有在CPU温度足够低的情况下才会起作用，而且我在测试中无法让酷睿i9-10900K达到5.3 GHz。
另一方面，AMD在其7 nm工艺上有很大的净空空间来继续推动更高的时钟速度，无论它们是保持还是增加核心计数。AMD负责计算和图形的执行副总裁里克·伯格曼(Rick Bergman)在一篇博客文章中写道，AMD发布了与第三代Ryzens几乎相同的第二个版本，这可能有点搅乱了局势，但它很快就会把Ryzen 4000移动处理器带到预置的台式机上，Zen 3也将按计划在今年晚些时候推出，AMD负责计算和图形的执行副总裁里克·伯格曼(Rick Bergman)在一篇博客文章中写道。
然后是苹果，它将正式发布配备自己定制ARM处理器的Mac电脑。该公司最终将完全淘汰英特尔-也许这些挫折与此有关。
note_en:
If you’ve been following along with Intel’s troubles moving away from its 14nm process to 10nm over the years, you probably won’t be surprised to learn that the company is now having trouble getting its 7nm process off the ground. On its   Q2 earnings call today, Intel revealed that it’s pushing back its previously planed 7nm rollout by six months—and that yields for the process are now a year behind schedule,   Tom’s Hardware reported. This means that Intel can’t produce 7nm chips in an economically viable way at the moment.
    Intel originally expected to catch up with AMD’s 7nm chips in 2021, but didn’t say when in 2021. With these new delays, that puts Intel’s 7nm chip debut in 2022, at the earliest. By then, AMD may already be on its Ryzen 6000 5nm chips on Zen 4 architecture, according to its roadmap—though that’s assuming AMD doesn’t run into any delays itself.
 However, there is some good news on the 10nm front. From Intel’s Q2 2020 press release, the company says it’s “accelerating its transition to 10nm products this year” and growing its portfolio of 10nm-based Intel Core processors. That includes its Tiger Lake chips and its first 10nm-based server CPU Ice Lake. Additionally, Intel said it “expects to deliver a new line of client CPUs (code-named Alder Lake), which will include its first 10nm-based desktop CPU, and a new 10nm-based server CPU (code-named Sapphire Rapids).”
  Intel originally announced its 10nm chips in 2015, but confirmed it was having yield issues and other problems that July. From there, its first 10nm Cannon Lake chips were supposed to debut in the second half of 2017, but by April 2018 Intel was still having poor yields, so still no chips. By 2019, Intel had refined its 10nm fabrication process, started seeing better results, and said it would be “on shelves” by holiday 2019. But we still don’t have consumer laptops or desktops with 10nm chips, aside from the processors with Iris Pro Graphics. The 10th generation of Intel mobile and desktop processors are still on 14nm. Will we see 10nm chips before 2020 is over? Based on what Intel said in its earnings call, it’s possible.
 Today Intel announced its new lineup of 10th generation U- and Y-series CPUs. Wait. Didn’t Intel…
 Read more
   But more importantly, Intel doesn’t expect its 10nm node to be as good as its 14nm. Intel’s   Chief Financial Officer George Davis admitted this at a March Morgan Stanley financial analyst event:
 “This just isn’t going to be the best node that Intel has ever had. It’s going to be less productive than 14 [nanometer], less productive than 22 [nanometer] … The fact is, like I said, it isn’t going to be as strong a node as people would expect from 14nm or what they’ll see in 7nm.”
  But will 7nm be a strong node? A lot of the same issues the company encountered with 10nm seem to be cropping up again, and if this is a case of history repeating itself, it could be 2025 until we see 7nm chips from Intel. That’s worst case scenario, and doesn’t sound like something Intel would stand for, especially given the heated competition from AMD at the moment. The company has also lost some   notable employees, like Jim Keller, most recently senior vice president of Intel’s silicon engineering group.
 But in the meantime, hopefully Intel’s 10nm chips are good enough to outpace its   10th-gen processors even slightly. There’s not much left to gain via the higher-end cores on the current 14nm node, unfortunately. The Core i9-10900K only has a 300 MHz higher max boost clock than the Core i9-9900K (5.3 GHz versus 5.0 GHz) due to Intel’s thermal velocity boost and turbo boost technologies—but those boosts only kick in if the CPU is at a low enough temperature, and I couldn’t get the Core i9-10900K to reach 5.3 GHz in my testing.
  On the flip side, AMD has a lot of headroom on its 7nm process to keep pushing for higher clock speeds, whether or not they keep or increase the core count. AMD may have muddied the water a bit by releasing a second,   near-identical version of its third-gen Ryzens, but it’s bringing its   Ryzen 4000 mobile processors to pre-built desktops soon, and Zen 3 is on track to launch later this year, Rick Bergman, AMD’s executive vice president of computing and graphics, wrote in a   blog post.
 And then there’s Apple, which is officially releasing Macs with its own custom   ARM processor. The company is eventually phasing out Intel altogether—and maybe   these stumbles had something to do with it.
title: 英特尔称其7纳米芯片延迟至少一年
title_en: Intel Says Its 7nm Chips Are Delayed By At Least a Year

note:
在苹果上个月的年度开发者大会上，该公司宣布，规模较小的开发者终于可以使用其“Find My”应用程序，此举从表面上看可能会安抚那些声称苹果权力太大的开发者。
根据“华盛顿邮报”获得的一份苹果秘密文件，事实证明，这一声明并不是看起来的那样。
Find My应用程序预装在苹果设备上，并显示使用iPhone和其他苹果产品的家庭成员的地理位置。苹果在大会上宣布，它将开放find My，以便竞争对手的公司能够使用它，使客户能够找到不是苹果制造的产品和小玩意儿。
从表面上看，向其他公司开放这一功能对Tile这样的开发者来说是橄榄枝。Tile是一家生产蓝牙标签的公司，可以挂在钥匙链上，也可以放在钱包里。现在，根据苹果公司的说法，像Tile这样的竞争对手也可以从Find My中受益。
但声明的细节-根据保密协议，所有开发商都被要求签署-讲述了一个不同的故事。“华盛顿邮报”获得的一份50页的PDF文件显示，苹果对消费者使用这款应用的方式进行了严格的限制。文件称，使用Find My定位设备的苹果客户将被禁止同时使用其他竞争对手的服务。
开发商说，这一举动是不同寻常的。客户通常可以选择使用多个应用程序来控制同一硬件设备。例如，蓝牙耳机既可以与Apple Music的流媒体服务一起使用，也可以与Spotify的流媒体服务一起使用。他们可以通过苹果的Home Kit或灯泡制造商提供的软件来控制他们的智能灯泡。他们可以使用Siri来控制智能恒温器，也可以使用亚马逊的Alexa。
苹果的决定突显出该公司愿意加强对其产品使用方式的控制，尽管眼尖的欧洲监管机构圈子里，美国议员准备在定于周一举行的有关其竞争行为的听证会上询问该公司执行长库克(Tim Cook)。
苹果严格控制着App Store，开发者每年在App Store的收入约为500亿美元，这还不包括广告收入。这家商店还充当公司庞大的保镖，挑选哪些竞争对手进入这个利润丰厚的俱乐部，以及他们进入俱乐部后可以做什么。这代表着该公司可以挖掘的最深脉络之一，以获得新的收入，而依赖App Store的移动应用程序制造商很容易受到这家iPhone制造商的侵蚀。例如，苹果利用这家商店寻找新想法，然后复制它们。
Tile通过App Store分发软件，以便其产品可以与iPhone兼容。该公司此前曾向欧洲监管机构和美国立法者表达过担忧，认为苹果对Tile的应用程序施加的限制使苹果的竞争产品具有优势。Tile的担忧是欧洲一个竞争监管机构关注的焦点，该机构正在调查苹果限制提供类似服务的竞争产品，同时给予自己的应用程序优势的举措是否违反了欧洲竞争法。
苹果发言人Alex Kirschner说，该软件可能对那些没有资源自行建立定位服务的小公司很有用。他说：“如果你是一个对进入搜索空间感兴趣的较小的参与者，而你还没有建立一个搜索网络，这可以让你做到这一点。”苹果否认其政策是反竞争的。
当苹果上个月宣布将允许外部访问Find My时，它上传了新软件的详细信息，这样开发者就可以下载它，并开始在未来某个时候正式推出“Find My Network”的小工具中实现它。
在此之前，开发人员必须签署一个额外的文档，称为“查找我的网络附件规范的有限许可”，这阻止了他们分享有关新规范的详细信息。据下载该文件的一名开发人员称，该文件威胁苹果公司将对个别开发者提起法律诉讼，因为他们谈论了这一问题。开发商分享了该文件的细节，因担心被苹果起诉而要求匿名。
这位开发者说，保密协议比苹果有时随预发行软件一起分发的协议更严格。
当新的find my网络在年底前后推出时，苹果的产品仍将拥有外部公司无法利用的内置优势。例如，每个苹果用户的位置都会自动与Find My服务共享。但像Tile这样的外部公司必须要求每个苹果用户允许才能获得他们的位置。去年，苹果取消了外部开发者要求客户在安装应用程序时“总是允许”共享他们的位置的能力。要找到能够正常工作的应用程序，需要始终在线位置共享。
Find My网络带来的另一个限制是，通过切断客户同时使用其他服务控制硬件设备的能力，苹果正在阻止他们连接到Android手机上的其他设备。这是因为Find My只在苹果设备上可用。谷歌旗下的安卓是苹果iOS操作系统的主要竞争对手。
苹果的find my可以随时使用iPhone的蓝牙天线和其他苹果硬件。在苹果自动切断蓝牙天线并阻止其工作之前，外部应用程序只能在一定的阈值内使用蓝牙天线。苹果没有告诉开发者这个门槛是什么。
蓝牙天线限制成为试图建立“接触追踪”应用程序以跟踪冠状病毒传播的国家和州的一个主要问题。由于苹果对蓝牙天线使用的限制，这些旨在追踪人们何时可能接触到病毒的应用程序无法正常工作。各国和各州恳求苹果公司允许完全使用蓝牙，但该公司拒绝了。
目前尚不清楚美国反垄断法是否足够强大，足以迫使苹果和其他国家改变行为。联邦法官对已有100年历史的反垄断法进行了解读，使其难以适用于现代公司。一些立法者希望通过更新法律来改变这一点。
西北大学凯洛格管理学院(Kellogg School Of Management At Northwest University)的技术与创新临床教授詹姆斯·康利(James Conley)说，“争论的地方在欧洲，而不是在美国。”
note_en:
During Apple’s annual developer’s conference last month, it announced that smaller developers would finally have access to its “Find My” app, a move that on the surface could appease developers who have asserted that Apple has too much power.
  It turns out the announcement was not what it seemed, according to a secret Apple document obtained by The Washington Post.
 The Find My app comes pre-installed on Apple devices and shows the geographic location of family members with iPhones and other Apple products. Apple announced at its conference that it was opening up Find My so that competing companies would be able to use it, enabling customers to locate products and gadgets that aren’t made by Apple.
  Ostensibly, opening the feature to other companies was an olive branch to developers like Tile, the maker of Bluetooth tags that can be attached to keychains or placed in wallets. Now, according to Apple, competitors like Tile could benefit from Find My, too.
  But the details of the announcement — kept secret by a confidentiality agreement all developers were required to sign — tell a different story. A 50-page PDF obtained by The Post shows Apple has placed strict restrictions on how consumers will be able to use the app. Apple customers who use Find My to locate a device will be barred from using other competing services simultaneously, the document says.
 The move is unusual, developers say. Customers generally have the choice of using multiple apps to control the same hardware device. For instance, a Bluetooth headset can be used with both Apple Music’s streaming service and Spotify’s. They can control their smart lightbulbs with Apple’s Home Kit or software offered by the manufacturer of the lightbulb. They can use Siri to control a smart thermostat, as well as Amazon’s Alexa.
  Apple’s decision highlights the company’s willingness to tighten its grip and control over how its products are used, even as hawk-eyed European regulators circle and U.S. lawmakers prepare to question the company’s chief executive, Tim Cook, at a  hearing scheduled for Monday about its competition practices.
   Apple keeps a tight grip over the App Store, where about  $50 billion is earned by developers every year, not including ad revenue. The store also acts as the company’s hulking bouncer, picking which competitors get into the lucrative club and what they can do once they’re inside. It represents one of the deepest veins the company can mine for new revenue, and makers of mobile apps that rely on the App Store are vulnerable to the iPhone maker’s encroachments. Apple uses the store, for instance, to  find new ideas and then copy them.
 Tile, which distributes software through the App Store so that its products will work with iPhones, has previously  voiced concerns to European regulators and U.S. lawmakers about restrictions Apple places on Tile’s app that gives Apple’s competing product an advantage. Tile’s concerns are a focus of a competition watchdog in Europe, which is investigating whether Apple’s moves to hamstring competing products that offer similar services while giving its own app an advantage violate European competition law.
 Apple spokesman Alex Kirschner said the software could be useful to smaller companies that don’t have the resources to build a location-finding service on their own. “If you were a smaller player interested in getting into the finding space and you haven’t built a finding network, this allows you to do that,” he said. Apple has denied that its policies are anticompetitive.
    When Apple announced last month that it would be allowing outside access to Find My, it uploaded the details of the new software so that developers could download it and begin working on implementing it in gadgets for the official launch of the “Find My network” sometime in the future.
 Before doing so, developers had to sign an additional document, called the “Limited License to Find My Network Accessory Spec,” which prevented them from sharing details about the new specification. The document threatened legal action by Apple against individual developers for speaking about it, according to a developer who downloaded the document. The developer shared the details of the document and spoke on the condition of anonymity for fear of being sued by Apple.
   That developer said the confidentiality agreement was stricter than the ones Apple sometimes distributes along with prerelease software.
    When the new  Find My network launches around the end of the year, Apple’s product will still have built-in advantages that outside companies cannot capitalize on. Every Apple user’s location is automatically shared with the Find My service, for instance. But outside companies, such as Tile, must ask each Apple user for permission to obtain their location. Last year, Apple took away the ability of outside developers to ask customers to “always allow” their location to be shared upon installing an app. Always-on location sharing is required for finding apps to work properly.
  Another limitation imposed by the Find My network is that, by cutting off the ability of customers to simultaneously use other services to control hardware devices, Apple is blocking them from connecting to other devices on Android phones. That’s because Find My is only available on Apple devices. Google-owned Android is the main competitor to Apple’s iOS operating system.
  Apple’s Find My can use the iPhone’s Bluetooth antenna and other Apple hardware whenever it wants. Outside apps must only use the Bluetooth antenna within certain thresholds, before Apple automatically cuts it off and prevents it from working. Apple does not tell developers what that threshold is.
 The Bluetooth antenna limitation became a major issue for countries and states trying to build “contact-tracing” apps to follow the spread of the coronavirus. Because of Apple’s limitations on Bluetooth antenna use, the apps, which were meant to track when people were potentially exposed to the virus, did not work properly. Countries and states  pleaded with Apple to allow full access to Bluetooth, but the company refused.
  It is unclear whether the U.S. antitrust laws are strong enough to force a change in behavior by Apple and other countries. Federal judges have interpreted 100-year-old antitrust laws in ways that make them difficult to apply to modern companies. Some lawmakers hope to change that with updated laws.
 “The place to argue this is in Europe and not here in the United States,” said James Conley, a clinical professor of technology and innovation at the Kellogg School of Management at Northwestern University.
title: 泄露的文件称，虽然苹果将允许第三方设备连接来查找我的，但它不会允许用户同时使用其他定位服务或应用程序
title_en: Leaked doc says that while Apple will let third-party devices connect to Find My, it won't allow users to use other locating services or apps simultaneously

这是一个关于一家软件公司如何通过将他们的电子邮件长度缩短90%来开始与8倍以上的用户对话的故事。您可以在不到一小时的时间内设置此方法的测试。
您可以为您的软件业务做的最有价值的事情之一就是与您的用户交谈。您可以帮助他们从您的软件中获得更多价值，了解他们如何使用您的产品，听取他们的反馈，并展示您的支持的质量和承诺。
然而，如果你曾经试图通过电子邮件与你的用户交谈，你可能会遇到一个问题：许多用户可能没有回应。
即使你遵循了电子邮件方面的所有最佳实践，真心想帮助他们，花了很长时间写一封个性化的电子邮件，人们仍然不会回复。这可能会非常令人沮丧。
Netlify--一个静态网站的平台--感受到了这种挫折感，所以他们请我来帮忙。
Netlify知道，如果新用户不开始使用该软件并从中获得价值，注册并不重要，因此Netlify向每一位新用户发送电子邮件，表示愿意提供帮助或回答任何问题。
从大多数标准来看，这封电子邮件都很好：它是个性化的，在一天内发送，由真人(他们的客户成功负责人)发送，没有华而不实的设计。
不幸的是，它的回复率只有1%。他们错过了一个巨大的机会，因为他们未能接触到每100名用户中的99名。
假设这是一个模板电子邮件，因为它的长度，因此不要相信提供帮助是真诚的。
我做的第一件事是删除了大部分电子邮件文本，并对原始…运行了A/B测试。
这封原始电子邮件有150多字，回复率为1%(每100个用户中有1个)。
这一变体有37个单词，回复率为4%(25个用户中有1个)。
对于几分钟的工作来说，这是一个非常好的结果。我本可以到此为止，但我想知道在看到回报递减之前，我还能走多远。
我从第一次测试中拿出了获胜的电子邮件，又删减了50%的文本，在保留电子邮件含义的同时尽可能多地去掉了，然后与之前的获胜者进行了测试。
这个版本只有14个字。其结果是回复率为8%(12个用户中有1个)。
将电子邮件文本再削减50%，使电子邮件的回复率比前一版本翻了一番。与原来相比，篇幅缩短了90%，回复率提高了700%。
想象一下，你可以让8倍以上的人加入你的软件，并从新用户那里听到8倍以上的反馈。这就是少说的力量。
接受你的欢迎邮件或登机电子邮件-如果你没有电子邮件，就从这里开始-并删掉任何不必要的文本。当你觉得你做完了，再拿出50%。最后，我运行A/B测试，测量对这封较短的电子邮件与您的原始邮件的正面回复。
告诉我你过得怎么样！如果你需要帮助，我随时可以咨询。

note_en:
This is a story of how a software company was able to start a conversation with 8x more of their users by cutting the length of their emails by 90%. You could set up a test of this method in less than an hour.
   One of the most valuable things you could do for your software business is talk to your users. You can help them get more value from your software, learn how they use your product, hear their feedback, and demonstrate the quality and commitment of your support.
 However, if you’ve ever tried to talk to your users by email, you probably ran into a problem:  Many users don’t respond.
 Even if you follow all the best practices around emails, genuinely want to help them, and spend a long time writing a personalized email, people still don’t respond. That can be very frustrating.
 Netlify— a platform for static sites—was feeling this frustration, so they brought me in to help.
 Knowing that signups don’t matter if new users aren’t starting to use and get value from the software, Netlify was emailing every new user with an offer to help or answer any questions.
 By most measures, the email was good: It was personalized, sent within a day, sent by a real person (their head of customer success), and had  no flashy design.
 Unfortunately it had a reply rate of just 1%. They were missing a huge opportunity by failing to reach 99 of every 100 users.
   Assuming it's a template email because of its length, and therefore don't believe the offer to help is sincere.
 The first thing I did was delete most of the email text and run an A/B test against the original…
   This original email had 150+ words and a reply rate of 1% (1 in 100 users).
  This variation had 37 words and resulted in a reply rate of 4% (1 in 25 users).
  That’s a terrific result for a few minutes of work. I could have stopped there, but wondered how far I could take this before seeing diminishing returns.
   I took the winning email from the first test and cut another 50% of the text, taking out as much as possible while retaining the email’s meaning, then tested it against the previous winner.
  This version has just 14 words. It resulted in a reply rate of 8% (1 in 12 users).
 Cutting the email text by another 50%  doubled the reply rates from the previous version. Compared to the original, the length was cut by 90% and reply rates increased by 700%.
  Imagine you could onboard 8x more people to your software and hear 8x more feedback from new users. That’s the power of saying less.
  Take your welcome or onboarding email— start here if you don’t have one—and cut out any text that isn’t necessary. When you feel you’re done, take out another 50%. Finally, run an A/B test and measure positive replies to this shorter email versus your original.
 Tell me how it goes for you! If you need help, I’m available for  consulting.

title: 简短的欢迎电子邮件收到的Netlify回复数量是Netlify的8倍(2017)
title_en: Short welcome emails got 8x more replies for Netlify (2017)

note:
每年，美国的人均生产量比大多数其他发达经济体都要高。2015年，美国实际人均GDP为5.6万美元。同年，经购买力调整后，德国的实际人均GDP仅为4.7万美元，法国和英国为4.1万美元，意大利仅为3.6万美元。
我能想到美国区别于其他工业经济体的10个特征，我在最近为美国国家经济研究局(National Bureau Of Economic Research)撰写的一篇文章中概述了这些特征，本文就是根据这篇文章改编的。
一种创业文化。美国的个人表现出创业和发展的愿望，以及承担风险的意愿。在美国文化中，失败并重新开始的惩罚较少。即使是上过大学或商学院的学生也表现出了这种创业愿望，而且这种愿望是自我强化的：像Facebook这样的硅谷成功激发了更多的创业精神。
支持创业的金融体系。美国拥有比欧洲国家更发达的股权融资体系，包括愿意为初创企业融资的天使投资者，以及非常活跃的风险资本市场，这有助于为这些公司的成长提供资金。我们还有一个分散的银行系统，包括7000多家小银行，为企业家提供贷款。
世界一流的研究型大学。美国大学生产了许多推动高科技创业的基础研究。教职员工和博士毕业生经常花时间与附近的初创公司打交道，而大学和企业的文化都鼓励了这种重叠。顶尖研究型大学吸引了来自世界各地的有才华的学生，其中许多人最终留在了美国。
劳动力市场通常将工人和工作联系起来，不受大型工会、国有企业或过于严格的劳动法规的阻碍。在美国私营部门的劳动力中，只有不到7%的人加入了工会，而且几乎没有国有企业。虽然美国确实对工作条件和招聘进行了监管，但这些规定远没有欧洲那么繁琐。因此，工人有更好的机会找到合适的工作，公司发现更容易创新，新公司发现更容易开始。
不断增长的人口，包括来自移民的人口。美国不断增长的人口意味着更年轻、更灵活、更容易培训的劳动力。尽管对进入美国的移民有限制，但也有特殊的规则，提供进入美国经济的渠道和获得公民身份(绿卡)的途径，基于个人人才和行业赞助。一个单独的“绿卡彩票”为渴望来美国的人提供了一条途径。这个国家吸引移民的能力一直是其繁荣的重要原因。
鼓励勤奋工作和长时间工作的文化(和税收制度)。美国员工平均每年工作1800小时，大大超过法国的1500小时和德国的1400小时(尽管没有香港、新加坡和韩国的2200多小时多)。一般来说，工作时间越长意味着生产越多，这意味着更高的实际收入。
使北美能源独立的能源供应。特别是天然气水力压裂技术为美国企业提供了充足且相对便宜的能源。
良好的监管环境。尽管美国的法规远非完美，但与欧洲国家和欧盟实施的法规相比，它们给企业带来的负担要小得多。
比其他工业国家规模小的政府。根据经合组织的数据，美国政府在联邦、州和地方各级的支出合计占GDP的38%，而德国、意大利和法国的相应数字分别为44%、51%和57%。其他国家的政府支出水平较高，不仅意味着税收占收入的比例更高，而且意味着更高的转移支付，从而降低了工作动机。美国人大量工作并不令人惊讶，他们有额外的动机这样做。
一种分权的政治体系，国家在其中相互竞争。各州之间的竞争鼓励创业和工作，各州通过完善法律规则和税收制度为企业和个人居民竞争。一些州没有所得税，并且有限制工会的劳动法。各州为本州学生提供学费低廉的高质量大学。他们在法律责任规则上也存在竞争。法律制度既吸引了新的企业家，也吸引了大公司。在高收入国家中，美国的政治分权程度可能是独一无二的。
美国会保持这些优势吗？约瑟夫·熊彼特在他1942年出版的“社会主义、资本主义和民主”一书中警告说，资本主义将会衰落和失败，因为资本主义的成功和对知识分子的批评将破坏资本主义繁荣所需的政治和智力环境。他认为，民选的社会民主党将创建一个限制企业家精神的福利国家。
尽管熊彼特的这本书是在他从欧洲搬到美国20多年后出版的，但他的警告似乎更适合今天的欧洲，而不是美国。福利国家在美国有所增长，但远低于欧洲。美国的知识氛围更支持资本主义。
如果熊彼特今天和我们在一起，他可能会指出，欧洲社会民主党的增长以及随之而来的福利国家的扩张，是欧洲工业国没有享受到美国那样强劲的经济增长的原因。
note_en:
Each year, the United States produces more per person than most other advanced economies. In 2015 real GDP per capita was  $56,000 in the United States. The real GDP per capita in that same year was only $47,000 in Germany, $41,000 in France and the United Kingdom, and just $36,000 in Italy, adjusting for purchasing power.
    I can think of 10 features that distinguish America from other industrial economies, which I outline in a  recent essay for the National Bureau of Economic Research, from which this article is adapted.
  An entrepreneurial culture. Individuals in the U.S. demonstrate a desire to start businesses and grow them, as well as a willingness to take risks. There is less penalty in U.S. culture for failing and starting again. Even students who have gone to college or a business school show this entrepreneurial desire, and it is self-reinforcing: Silicon Valley successes like Facebook inspire further entrepreneurship.
  A financial system that supports entrepreneurship. The U.S. has a more developed system of equity finance than the countries of Europe, including angel investors willing to finance startups and a very active venture capital market that helps finance the growth of those firms. We also have a decentralized banking system, including  more than 7,000 small banks, that provides loans to entrepreneurs.
  World-class research universities. U.S. universities produce much of the basic research that drives high-tech entrepreneurship. Faculty members and doctoral graduates often spend time with nearby startups, and the culture of both the universities and the businesses encourage this overlap. Top research universities attract talented students from around the world, many of whom end up remaining in the United States.
  Labor markets that generally link workers and jobs unimpeded by large trade unions, state-owned enterprises, or excessively restrictive labor regulations.  Less than 7% of the private sector U.S. labor force is unionized, and there are virtually no state-owned enterprises. While the U.S. does regulate working conditions and hiring, the rules are much less onerous than in Europe. As a result, workers have a better chance of finding the right job, firms find it easier to innovate, and new firms find it easier to get started.
  A growing population, including from immigration. America’s growing population means a younger and therefore more flexible and trainable workforce. Although there are restrictions on immigration to the United States, there are also special rules that provide access to the U.S. economy and a path for citizenship (green cards), based on individual talent and industrial sponsorship. A separate “green card lottery” provides a way for eager people to come to the United States. The country’s  ability to attract immigrants has been an important reason for its prosperity.
  A culture (and a tax system) that encourages hard work and long hours. The average employee in the United States works  1,800 hours per year, substantially more than the 1,500 hours worked in France and the 1,400 hours worked in Germany (though not as much as the 2,200+ in  Hong Kong,  Singapore, and  South Korea). In general, working longer means producing more, which means higher real incomes.
  A supply of energy that makes North America energy independent. Natural gas fracking in particular has provided U.S. businesses with plentiful and relatively inexpensive energy.
  A favorable regulatory environment. Although U.S. regulations are far from perfect, they are less burdensome on businesses than the regulations imposed by European countries and the European Union.
  A smaller size of government than in other industrial countries.  According to the OECD, outlays of the U.S. government at the federal, state, and local levels totaled 38% of GDP, while the corresponding figure was 44% in Germany, 51% in Italy, and 57% in France. The higher level of government spending in other countries implies not only a higher share of income taken in taxes but also higher transfer payments that reduce incentives to work. It’s no surprise that Americans work a lot; they have extra incentive to do so.
    A decentralized political system in which states compete. Competition among states encourages entrepreneurship and work, and states compete for businesses and for individual residents with their legal rules and tax regimes. Some states have no income taxes and have labor laws that limit unionization. States provide high-quality universities with low tuition for in-state students. They compete in their legal liability rules, too. The legal systems attract both new entrepreneurs and large corporations. The United States is perhaps unique among high-income nations in its degree of political decentralization.
  Will America maintain these advantages? In his 1942 book,  Socialism, Capitalism, and Democracy, Joseph Schumpeter warned that capitalism would decline and fail because the political and intellectual environment needed for capitalism to flourish would be undermined by the success of capitalism and by the critique of intellectuals. He argued that popularly elected social democratic parties would create a welfare state that would restrict entrepreneurship.
  Although Schumpeter’s book was published more than 20 years after he had moved from Europe to the United States, his warning seems more appropriate to Europe today than to the United States. The welfare state has grown in the United States, but much less than it has grown in Europe. And the intellectual climate in the United States is much more supportive of capitalism.
  If Schumpeter were with us today, he might point to the growth of the social democratic parties in Europe and the resulting expansion of the welfare state as reasons why the industrial countries of Europe have not enjoyed the same robust economic growth that has prevailed in the United States.
title: 为什么美国仍然比其他大国更富有
title_en: Why the U.S. Is Still Richer Than Every Other Large Country

note:
河马-一家专注于家庭保险的保险科技初创公司-在过去12个月中将毛保费增长至2.7亿美元后，以15亿美元的事后估值进行了1.5亿美元的融资。在这样的估值和调整前的保费规模下，与另一家刚刚上市的风险投资支持的保险科技初创公司Lemonade相比，河马超便宜。
该交易所探索初创企业、市场和资金。你可以每天早上在Extra Crunch上阅读它，或者在你的收件箱里免费接收它。注册Exchange时事通讯，从7月25日开始删除周六。
没有必要重新说明河马的估值以及私募市场对该公司的估值。但我们昨天的工作确实给了我们机会，让我们有机会对新保险领域的其他玩家做一些有趣的计算，即Root和MetroMile。使用财务申报数据和Pitchbook和Crunchbase的估值数据，我们可以摸索出这两家公司使用河马和柠檬水目前的溢价倍数值多少钱。
如果你不熟悉，我们现在看到的这些初创企业作为一个整体已经筹集了超过10亿美元的资金；风投们真的很信任它们。那么，它们的定价如何，以及它们如何退出，将有助于决定许多风险基金的结果。
那么，与Lemonade和Hippo相比，创业保险市场上的其他参与者在他们最后的私下价格上是便宜的吗？他们的风险投资人是否支付了过高的价格？让我们来看看吧。
note_en:
Hippo — an insurtech startup focused on home insurance — put together a $150 million round at a $1.5 billion post-money valuation after growing its gross written premium to $270 million “in the past 12 months.” At that valuation, and at pre-adjustment premium scale,  Hippo   is super-cheap compared to  Lemonade, another venture-backed insurtech startup that just went public.
  The Exchange explores startups, markets and money. You can read it  every morning on Extra Crunch, or receive it for free in your inbox. Sign up  for The Exchange newsletter, which drops Saturdays starting July 25.
  There’s no need to  relitigate Hippo’s valuation and how the private markets have valued the firm. But our work yesterday does give us the chance to do some fun math on other players in the neo-insurance space, namely,  Root and  MetroMile. Using data accrued from financial filings and valuation data from Pitchbook and Crunchbase, we can grok how much the two firms are worth using Hippo’s and Lemonade’s current premium multiples.
 If you aren’t familiar, the cohort of startups we’re looking at have raised well over $1 billion as a group; VCs  really believe in them. How they are priced then, and how they exit, will help determine the results of many a venture fund.
 So, are other players in the startup insurance market cheap at their last private price when compared to Lemonade and Hippo? Did their venture backers overpay? Let’s find out.
title: 保险科技初创公司是否被低估了？
